DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: ATTY ANDREW SCHER, 415.788.4545, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF DAVID KLONOFF;
ATTY CARY D. McREYNOLDS, CDM LAW, 925.385.8545, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to both causes of action. Defendant's demurrer to the first cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts that Defendant delivered a written letter to Plaintiff's counsel threatening to expose Plaintiff's alleged crime to the IRS, intending to use fear of exposure to get Defendant's divorce case to settle and to get money or property from Plaintiff. (See Plaintiff's Complaint 13-18.) Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to show that Defendant's conduct was outrageous, Defendant intended to cause Plaintiff emotional distress, and Defendant suffered emotional distress as the result of Defendant's conduct. (See Plaintiff's Complaint 20-24.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: ATTY ANDREW SCHER, 415.788.4545, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF DAVID KLONOFF;
ATTY CARY D. McREYNOLDS, CDM LAW, 925.385.8545, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CELINE TAMIR'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT AND RECOVER COSTS AND FEES UNDER CCP SECTION 425.16 is denied as to both causes of action. The evidence establishes that Defendant's alleged conduct does not arise from protected activity, as it is illegal extortion. (See Flatley v. Mauro, 39 Cal. 4th 299, 320, (2006).) Threats or accusations of violations of the law are sufficient to show the element of fear by induced threat under Cal. Penal Code. 519. (See Flatley, 39 Cal. 4th at 332.) Moreover, any party, who with the intent to extort, sends any letter implying threats of a crime under Cal. Penal Code 519, is punishable in the same manner as if such money or property were actually obtained by means of such threat. (See Cal. Penal Code 523.) The evidence shows that Defendant sent a written demand letter to Plaintiff's counsel on April 25, 2013, with the intent to extort Plaintiff by inducing fear by threat of exposing Plaintiff's crime to the Internal Revenue Service. (Declaration of McReynolds in Support of Special Motion to Strike and Recover Costs and Fees Under CCP Section 425,16, Exh. A, pgs. 7-8.) If the case did not settle, Defendant would also seek contribution fees from Plaintiff. (See Id.) Because Defendant has not made a prima facie showing that the alleged conduct arises out of protected activity, Plaintiff does not need to establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67. Plaintiff's request for attorney fees and costs is denied, as the evidence does not show that Defendant's special motion to was frivolous or solely intended to cause unnecessary delay. (See CCP 425.16.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.
